title: lepidium spathulatum
intro: Lepidium spathulatum, also known as water pepper, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Lepidium spathulatum include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion by steeping its leaves in hot water, and it can also be used in salves and ointments to promote wound healing.
